column1: # Album fixtures for the App Store demo server. Tab-separated. Single
# source: archive.org. Each row corresponds to one archive.org item;
# the fetcher pulls every VBR-MP3 file in that item and lays them out
# under <demo_media>/music/<artist>/<album>/<NN-track>.mp3 so
# MusicScannerAgent walks them naturally.
# Why archive.org-only: Musopen's catalog is now login-gated and
# their public download URLs return auth errors for non-browser
# clients. The Internet Archive's 78rpm + classical-recording
# collections cover the same PD ground (early jazz / blues /
# classical) and ship pre-derived VBR MP3s with id3 tags filled in.
# Columns:
#   archive_id      archive.org item identifier
#   mb_release_id   MusicBrainz release MBID. Blank lets the music
#                   scanner's heuristic match try; for demo
#                   determinism prefer to fill these in once you've
#                   confirmed the right MBID.
#   artist          Display artist (used as the artist subdir).
#   album           Display album title (used as the album subdir).
# Lines beginning with '#' or blank are ignored.
# Mix targets BPM bands the dance-preset advanced-search uses:
#   - Strauss waltzes  → Slow Waltz / Viennese Waltz presets
#   - 1920s big-band   → Foxtrot tempo
#   - tango            → Argentine Tango preset
# Plus the classical-staples baseline so the music landing page has a
# recognizable mix beyond dance-test material.
# Track count per row varies wildly — the Bach collection items have
# 18+ tracks each; the single-78 items have 1 track. Total fetched
# track count for this list is currently ~53.
